Title: Expression of the β-glucosidase gene Pgbglu-1 underpins natural resistance of white spruce against spruce budworm

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: active defence compounds against spruce budworm; retard larval development and increase mortality
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: acetophenone defence metabolism / acetophenone aglycon release pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: deglucosylation step releasing acetophenone aglycons from glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target products by releasing aglycons from stored glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a control point; glucoside precursor availability does not appear rate-limiting for aglycon release
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydrolyses the acetophenone glucosides picein and pungenin to release the aglycons piceol and pungenol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: preferentially expressed in foliage; much higher in resistant than non-resistant trees; higher in June than August; heritable higher expression in progeny of resistant trees
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: preferentially expressed in foliage; much higher in resistant than non-resistant trees; higher in June than August; heritable higher expression in progeny of resistant trees
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: comparison of resistant vs non-resistant mature trees; seasonal sampling (June and August); trees sampled years after spruce budworm outbreak; common garden trees without observed defoliation; progeny grown without spruce budworm exposure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes accumulation of biologically active acetophenone aglycons piceol and pungenol associated with natural resistance to spruce budworm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcriptome profiling; RT-qPCR; full-length cDNA cloning; sequence analysis; recombinant protein expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; enzyme assays with purified protein; LC-MS product identification; metabolite profiling; population correlation analysis; inheritance analysis in progeny
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray transcriptome profiling (GEO GSE57301); RNA-seq used for full-length transcript reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: high acetophenone aglycon levels may be toxic to producing tissues, suggesting need for glucosylation/storage and controlled release
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 1. Coding-sequence polymorphisms were not specific to resistant or non-resistant trees and did not affect conserved catalytic sites. 2. Protein contains predicted N-terminal signal peptide and catalytic glutamates E199 and E413. 3. Enzyme showed high specificity for picein compared with other related glucosides. 4. Trait appears constitutive rather than damage-induced under studied conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pgbglu-1 transcripts were ~770- to 1000-fold higher in resistant versus non-resistant white spruce foliage. 2. PgbGLU-1 hydrolysed picein and pungenin to produce piceol and pungenol, respectively. 3. Transcript abundance positively correlated with piceol and pungenol levels across years and in a natural population. 4. Higher expression in progeny of resistant trees indicates heritable variation linked to defence chemistry. 5. The gene functions as a key control point underpinning constitutive spruce budworm resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: use Pgbglu-1 transcript level, enzyme activity, and acetophenone aglycon accumulation as markers for selecting spruce budworm-resistant trees; prioritize regulatory alleles with high foliage expression; consider coupling with future glucosyltransferase knowledge and monitor possible self-toxicity/off-target defence costs
